description_long: This is an ornate, tall ceramic vase with a wide, flared mouth and a gently tapering body, finished in a warm beige and gold palette. The vase features two sculpted handles shaped like mythical creatures or lions, attached symmetrically on either side. Its surface is richly decorated with intricate gold geometric patterns and a central painted scene depicting a group of figures in traditional attire, likely inspired by East Asian art. The rim and base are accented with delicate gilded details, enhancing its luxurious appearance. The body of the vase is slightly bulbous, providing a generous interior cavity suitable for holding flowers or as a decorative centerpiece. The overall craftsmanship and embellishments suggest it is intended for display in elegant or ceremonial settings.
description: A tall, ornate ceramic vase with gold geometric patterns, sculpted animal handles, and a painted scene of figures in traditional attire. The vase has a flared rim and is richly decorated, exuding a luxurious, classical style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main painted scene of figures and both sculpted animal handles clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one sculpted animal handle in profile and the gold geometric patterning.
description_view_2: Rear view with the continuation of the painted scene and both handles visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other sculpted animal handle and the vase's decorative motifs.
attribute_pattern: gold geometric patterns and figurative scene
